keepers_cottage.enter: A small stone cottage. The fire went out. The last Keeper's journal lies open on the table.
keepers_cottage.examine_journal: The final entry reads: 'The mechanism won't hold. Three nights, three failures. It knows when I'm close.'
keepers_cottage.examine_fire: Cold ash. At least three days since it was lit.

lighthouse_base.enter: The lighthouse rises above you. The door is unlocked, but something about the threshold resists you.
lighthouse_base.examine_door: Salt-crusted iron hinges. Fresh scratch marks around the lock.

harbor.enter: The harbour is quiet. Too quiet for a working port. A single lantern burns at the harbormaster's office.
harbor.examine_boats: The boats are tied down. Supplies are still aboard.

village_square.enter: The village square. A few residents hurry past without making eye contact.
village_square.examine_well: The well has been sealed with planks. Nailed shut from above.

ruins.enter: Older than the village. Older, perhaps, than the island itself. The stones don't fit together right.
ruins.examine_stones: Maritime runes. But they predate any known maritime civilisation.

mechanism_room.enter: Gears the size of millstones. Whatever drove them once, it wasn't wind or water.

chapel.enter: St. Maret's Chapel. The doors are never locked. Someone has been lighting candles.

mill.enter: The old mill creaks in a wind that isn't there. The wheel hasn't turned in years.

forest_path.enter: The treeline begins here. Strange how the path seems different every time you walk it.

cliffside.enter: The cliff edge drops sharply to the cove below. At certain hours, something moves in the water.

archive_basement.enter: Row upon row of stone shelves. Whatever is catalogued here predates the village by centuries.

tidal_caves.enter: The tide retreats to reveal caves no one has mapped. The walls are covered in writing.

mechanism_room.enter: Gears the size of millstones. Whatever drove them once, it wasn't wind or water.

chapel.enter: St. Maret's Chapel. The doors are never locked. Someone has been lighting candles.

mill.enter: The old mill creaks in a wind that isn't there. The wheel hasn't turned in years.

forest_path.enter: The treeline begins here. Strange how the path seems different every time you walk it.

cliffside.enter: The cliff edge drops sharply to the cove below. At certain hours, something moves in the water.

archive_basement.enter: Row upon row of stone shelves. Whatever is catalogued here predates the village by centuries.

tidal_caves.enter: The tide retreats to reveal caves no one has mapped. The walls are covered in writing.

vision.loop5: You see the lighthouse as it was. Full of light. Full of people. They were not afraid then.
vision.loop10: A Keeper — not you — walks toward the mechanism room. She does not come back out.
vision.loop15: The thing beneath the island has a face. You have seen it before. In your reflection.

history_01.title: The First Keeper
history_01.body: Fragmentary records of the lighthouse's first appointed keeper, whose name has been struck from the official register.

history_02.title: Founding Charter
history_02.body: A partial transcription of the island's founding charter. Several clauses have been deliberately obscured.

history_03.title: The Great Storm
history_03.body: Accounts of the storm of 1743 diverge significantly. Some speak of a light that never went out.

history_04.title: The Survey
history_04.body: An old surveyor's report noting geological anomalies beneath the ruins that do not appear in the final submitted version.

history_05.title: Keeper's Log: Year One
history_05.body: The first keeper wrote with pride. By year's end the handwriting had changed.

history_06.title: The Rotation
history_06.body: Lighthouse keepers were meant to rotate every three years. Records show some stayed considerably longer.

history_07.title: Last Official Correspondence
history_07.body: The final letter to the mainland authority. It was never answered.

history_08.title: The Parish Record
history_08.body: Births and deaths recorded by the chapel. The death entries stop abruptly in a particular year.

history_09.title: The Annexe
history_09.body: A small room behind the chapel altar is not on any official plan of the building.

history_10.title: Oral Histories
history_10.body: Three contradictory accounts of what happened when the last Keeper disappeared. All three are probably true.

occult_01.title: The Binding Marks
occult_01.body: The symbols carved into the ruins predate any known written language. They share structural features with musical notation.

occult_02.title: Resonance Theory
occult_02.body: Whoever carved these marks understood something about sound, light, and attention that we do not.

occult_03.title: The Loop Index
occult_03.body: A set of tally marks at the ruins that no one has been able to explain. The count is very high.

occult_04.title: The Old Name
occult_04.body: The entity beneath has a name in the old script. It roughly translates as 'the one that remembers the drowned.'

occult_05.title: The Cliff Vigil
occult_05.body: Someone has been keeping watch from the cliffside for a very long time. The worn stone suggests decades.

occult_06.title: The Feeding Pattern
occult_06.body: The entity does not sleep. It simply waits for the beam to align.

occult_07.title: Echo Residue
occult_07.body: Echoes of prior keepers concentrate near the cliff edge. The resonance is measurable if you know what to measure.

occult_08.title: The Tide Script
occult_08.body: The writing on the cave walls is rewritten by the tides. Something underneath keeps restoring it.

occult_09.title: The Deep Chamber
occult_09.body: Behind the main cave there is a chamber that only opens under specific tidal conditions.

occult_10.title: The Final Binding
occult_10.body: The original binding required three keepers. The seal has never been properly completed.

maritime_01.title: Shipping Manifests
maritime_01.body: The last recorded arrival was seventeen months ago. The manifest lists cargo that was never offloaded.

maritime_02.title: The Harbour Depth
maritime_02.body: The harbour was dredged three times in the last century. Each time they found something and stopped.

maritime_03.title: Navigation Anomalies
maritime_03.body: Compasses behave strangely within half a league of the island. The lighthouse beam does not correct for this.

maritime_04.title: The Wrecks
maritime_04.body: Twelve wrecks are on record. Local lore suggests there are forty more that were never reported.

maritime_05.title: The Harbormaster's Book
maritime_05.body: Silas maintains meticulous records. The three-month gap is filled with a single word, repeated.

maritime_06.title: Cave Tidal Charts
maritime_06.body: Handmade charts of tidal patterns in the caves. More precise than anything the Navy has produced.

maritime_07.title: The Swimmer's Path
maritime_07.body: A series of markers leads from the cave entrance toward deeper water. They were not made by human hands.

maritime_08.title: Bioluminescence Log
maritime_08.body: Notes on unusual light emissions from the cave water. The pattern matches the lighthouse beam exactly.

maritime_09.title: The Current
maritime_09.body: There is a persistent current that runs from the tidal caves toward open water. It flows uphill in places.

maritime_10.title: The Missing Boats
maritime_10.body: Three fishing boats are known to have entered the caves voluntarily. None returned.

ecology_01.title: The Forest Edge
ecology_01.body: The treeline has moved. Old maps show the forest beginning half a mile further from the lighthouse.

ecology_02.title: Fungal Network
ecology_02.body: The root network beneath the forest path is unlike anything documented. It pulses.

ecology_03.title: The Boundary Plants
ecology_03.body: Certain plants grow only at the exact perimeter of the lighthouse beam's reach. Nowhere else.

ecology_04.title: Animal Behaviour
ecology_04.body: No birds nest within the beam's range. Those that fly through it do not fly straight afterward.

ecology_05.title: The Path Changes
ecology_05.body: The forest path reroutes itself. Locals accept this as normal. It is not normal.

ecology_06.title: Cliff Flora
ecology_06.body: The plants growing from the cliff face have no known species classification. They flower at night.

ecology_07.title: The Lichen
ecology_07.body: A specific lichen pattern covers the south face of the cliff. Under magnification it resolves into text.

ecology_08.title: Sea Life Concentration
ecology_08.body: Marine life clusters beneath the cliff in numbers that shouldn't be sustainable. They are not feeding.

ecology_09.title: The Mill Mold
ecology_09.body: A species of mold is consuming the mill's timbers. It has the structural properties of bone.

ecology_10.title: Grain Mutations
ecology_10.body: The grain stored in the mill has been growing. In the dark. Without soil.

alchemy_01.title: The Preparation
alchemy_01.body: A formula for a compound that, when burned, produces a light visible only to those who have died and returned.

alchemy_02.title: Memory Distillation
alchemy_02.body: Notes on extracting the residue of repeated experiences. The process requires a loop to be in progress.

alchemy_03.title: The Anchor Compound
alchemy_03.body: A substance described as capable of anchoring an echo to a specific location. Ingredients: one is a person.

alchemy_04.title: Resonance Amplifiers
alchemy_04.body: Small objects that increase the sympathetic resonance between two beings. The Archivist has several.

alchemy_05.title: Chapel Herbs
alchemy_05.body: Several unusual herbs grow in the chapel garden. They are not native to this climate. They were planted deliberately.

alchemy_06.title: The Candle Formula
alchemy_06.body: The candles in the chapel are not ordinary wax. The smoke carries encoded information.

alchemy_07.title: Sacramental Chemistry
alchemy_07.body: The original chapel rites were pharmaceutical as well as spiritual. The two were not distinguished.

alchemy_08.title: The Suppressor
alchemy_08.body: A compound that prevents loop-memory from surfacing. Someone has been adding it to the village water supply.

alchemy_09.title: Antidote Notes
alchemy_09.body: Incomplete notes on reversing the suppressor compound. The last page is missing.

alchemy_10.title: The Final Reagent
alchemy_10.body: The one ingredient that cannot be synthesised. It must be freely given.

cartography_01.title: The True Map
cartography_01.body: A map of the island that shows locations which do not appear to exist. Several are marked as dangerous.

cartography_02.title: Sightline Analysis
cartography_02.body: The lighthouse was positioned to create specific sightlines to every significant location on the island.

cartography_03.title: The Beam's Reach
cartography_03.body: A precise calculation of the lighthouse beam's maximum reach under various atmospheric conditions.

cartography_04.title: Underground Map
cartography_04.body: A partial map of what lies beneath the island. The lower levels are marked with a symbol meaning do not continue.

cartography_05.title: The Cliff Survey
cartography_05.body: A survey of the cliff face noting entry points. Several are accessible only at specific phases of the tide.

cartography_06.title: The Offshore Markers
cartography_06.body: Submerged markers at specific distances from the cliff. Their purpose is not navigational.

cartography_07.title: The Dead Zone
cartography_07.body: A region of sea between the cliff and the nearest shipping lane where instruments fail completely.

cartography_08.title: Village Original Plan
cartography_08.body: The original plan of the village differs from what was built. The deviations are not random.

cartography_09.title: The Routes
cartography_09.body: A map of paths through the village and island with markings showing which are safe at which times.

cartography_10.title: Overlay Analysis
cartography_10.body: When all known maps are overlaid, a pattern emerges in the white space between them.

linguistics_01.title: Pre-Settlement Script
linguistics_01.body: A script predating the settlement of the island by at least four centuries. It uses the lighthouse as a reference point.

linguistics_02.title: The Archive Catalogue
linguistics_02.body: A catalogue of what the archive once contained. Most items are listed as removed or destroyed.

linguistics_03.title: Translation Notes
linguistics_03.body: Maren's personal translation notes. The margin comments are more revealing than the translations.

linguistics_04.title: The Cipher
linguistics_04.body: A cipher used in the more sensitive archive documents. The key is a number that changes each loop.

linguistics_05.title: Comparative Glossary
linguistics_05.body: A glossary comparing the old script to modern terms. The word for keeper and the word for sacrifice are the same.

linguistics_06.title: Ruin Inscription A
linguistics_06.body: The longest continuous inscription at the ruins. It appears to be instructions, written in the imperative.

linguistics_07.title: Ruin Inscription B
linguistics_07.body: A shorter inscription, written in a different hand. It is a response to Inscription A. It says no.

linguistics_08.title: The Name Tablets
linguistics_08.body: Stone tablets listing names. Some names appear on multiple tablets, in different hands, separated by what appear to be centuries.

linguistics_09.title: Chapel Marginalia
linguistics_09.body: Tiny writing in the margins of the chapel's record books. It is written by someone with access to the old script.

linguistics_10.title: The Repeating Phrase
linguistics_10.body: A phrase that appears in every document in the archive, in every language and script. It means approximately: it is not finished.
